item.title: GCP Filestore
item.description: Overview File-based storage NAS Provision in zone—access via VPC using NFSv3 Use case: lift-and-shift migrations Read-only snapshots supported Tiers Tier Capacities Use cases Basic HDD: 1 – 63.

item.title: GCP Local SSDs
item.description: Overview Fastest block storage solution Attached directly to Google Compute Engine VMs Zonal 375 GB disks Expandable to 3, 6 or 9 TBs—combined into a single logical volume IOPs increase with disk size Data encrypted at rest Data lost when VM stops Best for transient data—media rendering, caches, analytics Can survive live migration References GCP Object and File Storage

item.title: GCP Object and File Storage
item.description: Options Local SSDs Persistent Disks Filestore Cloud Storage Storage Solution Selection References

item.title: GCP Persistent Disks
item.description: Overview Disks independent of Compute Engine instance Data available ofter VM is shutdown Durable—data distributed across physical disks (99.9999%) Secure—encrypted at rest and transit Zonal Vs Regional Persistent Disks Zonal Regional Single Zone Two zones in same region Standard, balanced, SSD, extreme Standard, balanced, SSD Snapshots and boot disks Snapshots only Can resize capacity, throughput and IOPs Can resize capacity only References GCP Object and File Storage

item.title: Google BigQuery
item.description: Overview Data warehouse Serverless Multi-regional SQL column store Terabytes to petabytes ML and BI Real-time analytics Automatic replication, 7 day storage of changes References GCP Big Data Services AI ML Lifecycle

item.title: Google Cloud Dataflow
item.description: Overview Serverless—managed service, horizontal scaling Batch and streaming with a single model Often not the case Apache Beam Open source Beam SDK—Java, Python, Go (not currently supported) Dataflow is the backend for the Beam API References GCP Big Data Services Data Lifecycle AI ML Lifecycle

item.title: Google Cloud Dataprep
item.description: Overview Explore, clean, prepare for analysis and ML Can detect schemas—data types, joins, anomalies Prediction of next transformation Transformation functions, e.

item.title: Google Cloud Dataproc
item.description: Overview Map/Reduce—Apache Spark and Hadoop Backed by Zonal VMs Can use preemptible VMs—reduce cost One VM must be non-preemptible References GCP Big Data Services Data Lifecycle AI ML Lifecycle

item.title: Google Cloud Storage
item.description: Overview Infinitely scalable Highly durable (99.9999999%—eleven 9s) Unstructured data—images, videos etc. Data stored in buckets—regional or multi-regional Folder structure Object versioning—mutable files Permissions: bucket or object level Lifecycle management Rules to move objects between storage classes, or delete Storage Classes Class Usage Standard Most frequency accessed data Nearline Data accessed once per month Coldline Data accessed once per quarter Archive Data accessed once per year or less References GCP Object and File Storage
